name: Avenc des Mort
starting_point: Na Burguesa viewpoint, next to the monument of Our Lady of Peace (Génova)
coordinates_utm: DATUM EUROPEAN 1950: 462539 E, 4379714 N. DATUM ETRS89: 462445 E, 4379509 N
equipment: 2 carabiners, 2 ropes of 25 m, 2 slings (one for a deviation, one for the rebelay)
description: One of the most beautiful caves in the Serra de Na Burguesa despite its modest -30.5 m depth. Features a scenic 12 m aerial descent and richly decorated chambers with eccentric stalactites, numerous curtain formations, and hidden alcoves. In 1978, human remains were found of a person who apparently died of starvation. The visit can be combined with the nearby Cova des Ribellet.
full_description:
Much less well-known than other chasms in this mountain range such as the Avenc de s'Embut or the Avenc de sa Moneda, the Avenc des Mort is without a doubt one of the most beautiful cavities found in the Serra de Na Burguesa, despite not being a cave of large dimensions or even great depth at -30.5 m. Nevertheless, it meets the necessary conditions for spending a good while caving, where we can enjoy a beautiful descent into its depths in which we will perform a 12-meter aerial traverse, as well as being able to scrutinize beautiful hidden corners and spending hours firing our camera.

The installation can be set up on a pine tree near the larger of the two mouths the chasm has, then placing a deviation on a wild olive tree at the head of the ramp that will allow us to access the shaft after installing a first and last rebelay on formations securely. The descent, in a beautiful aerial position, will deposit us first on a stalagmitic mass and shortly after on unstable ground due to the large number of rocks that cover the floor of the cavity.

As soon as we free ourselves from the rope, we can begin to marvel at everything that surrounds us, which is a great deal since the lithochemical process has covered this place with all types of formations.

We can begin the visit by descending along an unstable ramp until reaching the deepest part of the cavity where there are beautifully adorned corners with small gours (dry at the time of my visit) near the place where the bone remains of a goat are found, which sought this corner to bid farewell to this world.

To access the most hidden areas of the cavity, one must climb a stalagmitic mass which poses no great risk if done carefully; the effort will be rewarded by what we will find in this place, from eccentric stalactites to a great concentration of curtains, in addition to allowing us to observe how the natural light entering from the outside illuminates the highest areas of the cavity.

In 1978, the remains of a corpse were found who apparently died of starvation, still preserving remains of their clothing (BARCELÓ, 1998). Said remains were surely removed from the place since I only found animal remains that over time have fallen into the interior.

In summary, a good option for spending a few hours inside, which can be complemented for the day with a visit to the Cova des Ribellet which is very nearby (462655 4379752, DATUM EUROPEAN 1950) or any of the many cavities in the area.
